user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Azure Ridge Partners. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 12:35 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 17:01 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:12:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:19:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:25:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
